Itemized  ·  Compare  ·  Whittier Hospital Medical Center vs Adventist Health Simi Valley

Whittier Hospital Medical Center vs Adventist Health Simi Valley.

Side-by-side prices for 18 procedures both hospitals publish, plus CMS quality ratings and metro context. Pulled from each hospital's federally-mandated price transparency file. Whittier Hospital Medical Center is cheaper on more procedures (14 vs 3).

Whittier Hospital Medical Center
Whittier, CA
AHMC Healthcare
2/5 CMS
Cheaper on 14 of 18 procedures
vs.
Adventist Health Simi Valley
Simi Valley, CA
Adventist Health
2/5 CMS
Cheaper on 3 of 18 procedures

Head-to-head, by procedure.

Procedure Whittier Adventist Cheaper
Brain MRI without contrast $593 $817 Whittier ↓
Brain MRI with and without contrast $782 $1,282 Whittier ↓
Lumbar spine MRI without contrast $529 $886 Whittier ↓
Knee/lower-extremity MRI without contrast $494 $585 Whittier ↓
CT abdomen and pelvis with contrast $834 $1,205 Whittier ↓
Abdominal ultrasound, complete $173 $414 Whittier ↓
Mammogram, screening $46 $111 Whittier ↓
Chest X-ray, single view $50 $120 Whittier ↓
Comprehensive metabolic panel $60 $26 Adventist ↓
Lipid panel $50 $33 Adventist ↓
CBC with differential $34 $19 Adventist ↓
TSH (thyroid stimulating hormone) $21 $46 Whittier ↓
Hemoglobin A1c $20 $24 Whittier ↓
Urinalysis $11 $15 Whittier ↓
Echocardiogram, complete with Doppler $348 $1,006 Whittier ↓
Thyroid ultrasound $183 $307 Whittier ↓
Prostate biopsy — $386 tie
Carotid duplex ultrasound $189 $467 Whittier ↓

Patient experience.

CMS HCAHPS patient survey, period ending 03/31/2025. Bold = higher score.

Measure Whittier Adventist
HCAHPS overall star rating 3/5 3/5
Would definitely recommend 59% 69%
Hospital rating 9 or 10 of 10 63% 68%
Nurses always communicated well 74% 76%
Doctors always communicated well 75% 70%
Given clear info about recovery 83% 87%
Room and bathroom always clean 72% 80%
Staff always explained meds 63% 56%
Quiet at night, always 52% 51%

Add your insurance.

Cash-pay is one number. With your insurance plan, the actual price differs. Pick your insurer in the comparison tool to see plan-specific rates at both hospitals.

Open comparison →

How to read this comparison.

The cash-pay price is what an uninsured patient would be charged at each hospital. It's the cleanest apples-to-apples comparison because it doesn't depend on your insurance plan.

The CMS rating is the federal quality composite, built from ~50 measures spanning safety, mortality, readmission, patient experience, and timeliness. A 5-star hospital may not be the best at every procedure, and a 3-star hospital can have a strong specific service line. Treat the rating as one input, not the answer.

For your specific insurance plan, prices can shift dramatically. Some hospitals negotiate steep discounts with one insurer and not another. Always check the plan-specific rate before you book.

Data sources: CMS Hospital Price Transparency rule (45 CFR 180.50). CMS Hospital Care Compare (xubh-q36u). CMS HCAHPS patient survey (dgck-syfz). Last refresh: 2026-04-27.
A consumer reading of CMS-mandated MRF data. Not medical or financial advice. Itemized · itemized.health
